So, the ambiance, the food, and the drinks were all delicious. But I can’t get over a pretty serious deception….

We ordered several glasses of Prosecco and received a sparkling rosé. We casually commented that we didn’t know that there are rosé proseccos, but we’re not experts and we were distracted by fun conversation with friends. Later, we wanted more of the same and decided to order a bottle. And this is when we found out that they’d been knowingly serving us a Spanish rosé sparkling (cava) and passing it off as the Prosecco that we’d ordered. They got caught only because we ordered the bottle and they had to bring it to the table and couldn’t hide what they’d done. 

Look, it wasn’t expensive wine. But that’s not the point!  And it’s very likely that the wines were of equal value - that’s what the nervous bartender remarked when he brought the bottle to the table. Most of us weren’t even aware of what was happening because we were busy talking. But my husband told us later what they’d done.  VERY BAD FORM. NOT OK BEHAVIOR. If you order wine you should ask them to pour it at the table.
